steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice best describes the overall structure of the passage, which moves from general context to a specific study to a finding.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'research team's reaction': invents an emotional response the passage never shows. ✗
  • Choice B — 'historical significance of the Darlington': mischaracterizes the passage's focus; the wreck is a case study, not the subject. ✗
  • Choice C — 'construction details and causal claims': fabricates information not in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — 'general interest, specific study, key finding': accurately tracks all three sequential elements of the passage. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
